User Story 1: As a new user, I can create an account.
User Story 2: As a new user, I can choose my role during sign-up.

User Story 1: As a user, I can log in with valid credentials.
User Story 2: As a user, I get clear error messages for failed login.

User Story 1: As a user, I stay logged in safely while active.
User Story 2: As a user, I can log out from any page.

User Story 1: As a data subject, I can select what data can be shared.
User Story 2: As a data subject, I can set how long sharing is allowed.

User Story 1: As an admin, I can enforce role-based access.
User Story 2: As an admin, I can enforce purpose-based access checks.

User Story 1: As a privacy officer, I can revoke consent immediately.
User Story 2: As a privacy officer, I can block new access after revocation.

User Story 1: As a clinician, I can view clinical and work context together.
User Story 2: As an HR manager, I can view the same profile with allowed fields.

User Story 1: As a coordinator, I can create a case record.
User Story 2: As a coordinator, I can move a case from intake to closure.

User Story 1: As a user, I can view key events in time order.
User Story 2: As a user, I can filter timeline events by type.

User Story 1: As an employee, I can submit screening answers.
User Story 2: As an employee, I can receive risk and severity results.

User Story 1: As a clinician, I can submit confirmed labels.
User Story 2: As a clinician, I can override AI results with a reason.

User Story 1: As governance staff, I can export screening data.
User Story 2: As governance staff, I can export clinician label data in one file.

User Story 1: As calibration staff, I can change model thresholds.
User Story 2: As calibration staff, I can enforce minimum specificity rules.

User Story 1: As an HR manager, I can create an accommodation plan.
User Story 2: As an HR manager, I can link plan items to clinical needs.

User Story 1: As HR, I can map job demands to cognitive requirements.
User Story 2: As HR, I can use this map for role-fit checks.

User Story 1: As a manager, I can track accommodation usage.
User Story 2: As a manager, I can review effectiveness over time.

User Story 1: As the platform, I can run workflow steps across services.
User Story 2: As the platform, I can track each step status.

User Story 1: As a coordinator, I can request recommendations from clinical inputs.
User Story 2: As a coordinator, I can include HR inputs in the same request.

User Story 1: As a coordinator, I can detect conflicts between recommendations.
User Story 2: As a coordinator, I can see conflict details clearly.

User Story 1: As a board member, I can compare intervention options.
User Story 2: As a board member, I can view trade-offs side by side.

User Story 1: As an analyst, I can view alignment and latency KPIs.
User Story 2: As an analyst, I can view outcome KPIs for reports.

User Story 1: As governance staff, I can monitor model drift.
User Story 2: As governance staff, I can monitor fairness alerts.

User Story 1: As governance staff, I can see why a recommendation was made.
User Story 2: As governance staff, I can review explanation records later.

User Story 1: As an analytics lead, I can analyze by condition and severity.
User Story 2: As an analytics lead, I can analyze by role group.

User Story 1: As a clinician, I receive important event alerts.
User Story 2: As an HR manager, I receive important event alerts for my role.

User Story 1: As a user, I can view pending review tasks.
User Story 2: As a user, I can view pending approval and follow-up tasks.

User Story 1: As governance staff, I can define escalation rules.
User Story 2: As governance staff, I can apply rules to unresolved critical cases.

User Story 1: As an integrator, I can use versioned API contracts.
User Story 2: As an integrator, I can use matching schema files.

User Story 1: As operations staff, I can publish domain events.
User Story 2: As operations staff, I can consume domain events reliably.

User Story 1: As the platform, I can sync with EHR systems.
User Story 2: As the platform, I can sync with HRIS systems.

User Story 1: As a user, I can access my role page after login.
User Story 2: As a user, I see employee, clinician, HR, or calibration views.

User Story 1: As an employee, I can complete screening step by step.
User Story 2: As an employee, I can review answers before submit.

User Story 1: As a clinician, I can review case evidence in one page.
User Story 2: As a clinician, I can submit labels from the same page.

User Story 1: As HR, I can manage accommodation plans.
User Story 2: As HR, I can manage role-fit plans from one dashboard.

User Story 1: As leadership, I can view KPI dashboards.
User Story 2: As leadership, I can view calibration dashboards in one place.

User Story 1: As compliance staff, I can log every data access event.
User Story 2: As compliance staff, I can capture user, purpose, time, and case ID.

User Story 1: As security staff, I can encrypt data in transit and at rest.
User Story 2: As security staff, I can rotate encryption keys on schedule.

User Story 1: As a privacy officer, I can set retention rules by data type.
User Story 2: As a privacy officer, I can enforce deletion rules by data type.

User Story 1: As DevOps, I can run tests and contract checks in CI.
User Story 2: As DevOps, I can run security scans in CI gates.

User Story 1: As SRE, I can monitor traces, errors, and latency.
User Story 2: As SRE, I can monitor backups and recovery readiness.

Epic: run cohort analysis by condition, severity, role, and intervention type
Feature: As analytics lead, I can run cohort analysis by condition, severity, role, and intervention type.
